generatedFrom: manual local source review, existing ingested artifacts, and online source discovery on 2026-05-17
copyrightPolicy: Use online and local sources for measured analysis, source manifests, contact sheets, labels, and derived metrics. Avoid committing full third-party gameplay videos or raw copyrighted audio unless explicitly approved.

artifacts.title: Bandai Namco Galaga Web history/game page
artifacts.targetUse: Official grounding for the game identity: formation entry, three enemy roles, tractor beam, dual fighter, challenging stages, and depth over repeat play.
artifacts.currentUse: Known to the team but not yet represented as a formal source artifact.
artifacts.missingWork: Capture a cited official-source summary into the project docs and use it as high-level design grounding rather than a numeric movement target.

artifacts.title: Galaga 1981 Namco/Midway manual
artifacts.targetUse: Rules, scoring, challenge-stage structure, capture behavior, bonus logic, presentation flow, and official cabinet/operator framing.
artifacts.currentUse: Manual observations already support challenge-stage and scoring rules.
artifacts.missingWork: Promote richer manual-derived assertions into structured per-rule artifacts so checks can cite rule IDs instead of prose notes.

artifacts.title: Galaga (1981) - Gameplay Arcade - Snake Latino
artifacts.targetUse: Concrete visual timing reference for early normal-stage entry, first challenge arrival, early challenge result flow, and early capture pressure.
artifacts.currentUse: This is the current strongest media-backed path reference. It has 10 extracted windows and validated path labels, including 4 accepted challenge-entry labels.
artifacts.missingWork: It is only a 360p early-window corpus. Add complete challenge-stage windows and later challenge stages beyond Challenge 3 before using it as the sole movement target.

artifacts.title: GamesDatabase Galaga page
artifacts.targetUse: Readable screen-surface examples and rule summaries: title, game-over, high-score, cabinet/artwork, challenge-stage non-combat rule, transforms, and alien roles.
artifacts.currentUse: Referenced informally for rule and screen-surface comparison.
artifacts.missingWork: Create a screen-surface target pack for title, high-score, game-over, and challenge-result surfaces with screenshot provenance and visual conformance notes.

artifacts.title: StrategyWiki Galaga walkthrough
artifacts.targetUse: Player-experience interpretation of entrance patterns, challenge-stage positioning, perfect-bonus learning, capture/rescue tactics, and transform progression.
artifacts.currentUse: Useful for qualitative player-meaning language, not yet a scored target.
artifacts.missingWork: Extract non-numeric expectations into designer/player meaning rows, then use measured media windows for actual path scoring.

artifacts.title: Galaga Collection Fandom challenging-stage page
artifacts.targetUse: Supplemental challenge-stage cadence and set-point totals.
artifacts.currentUse: Useful cross-check only; lower authority than manual or measured video.
artifacts.missingWork: Use only as a secondary consistency check against manual and captured gameplay.

artifacts.title: Arcade Game Series Galaga perfect challenge-stage guides
artifacts.targetUse: Late challenge-stage progression, perfect-bonus examples, and novelty enemy appearances around stages 11, 15, 19, 23, 27, and 31.
artifacts.currentUse: Identifies the late challenge stages that matter most for the next media capture pass.
artifacts.missingWork: Acquire lawful gameplay windows for the late challenge stages and promote per-group labels. Use the guides only to prioritize stage windows and expected novelty, not as pixel/motion ground truth.

artifacts.title: Arcade Longplay Galaga by World of Longplays
artifacts.targetUse: Long-run gameplay reference for later stages, challenge-stage variation, and progression beyond early windows.
artifacts.currentUse: Discovered as a likely useful longplay source through third-party references, but not yet ingested.
artifacts.missingWork: Find the exact source URL, record provenance, extract later challenge-stage windows, and label at least Challenges 4-8.

artifacts.title: Controlled local arcade/MAME-style capture
artifacts.targetUse: Repeatable clean capture of exact challenge-stage windows with consistent frame rate, native aspect, known input, and reproducible timestamps.
artifacts.currentUse: Not currently used. This is the best future route for high-precision stage-by-stage measurement if lawful source setup is available.
artifacts.missingWork: Set up an approved capture source, record source manifest, capture challenges 1-8, and extract trajectories/contact sheets with the same harness shape used for existing media.

artifacts.title: Galaga inferred sprite target models
artifacts.targetUse: Static sprite target comparison for player fighter, dual fighter, boss, butterfly, bee, and specialty challenge families.
artifacts.currentUse: Static sprite comparisons are active and measured, but motion is intentionally unscored.
artifacts.missingWork: Add active sprite motion windows for flapping, pulsing, dive/rotation, capture/rescue, and carried-fighter transitions.

artifacts.title: Galaga reference audio cue library
artifacts.targetUse: Reference cue comparison for shots, impacts, explosions, capture/rescue, challenge transition/results/perfect, stage start, and game over.
artifacts.currentUse: Audio reference comparison is active and has extensive candidate history.
artifacts.missingWork: Improve scoring stability and segment-boundary strategy before promoting more runtime audio changes.
